homepage.heroHeadline: Landscaping Tailored to Portland’s Unique Climate
homepage.heroSubline: Expert care for gardens thriving in Oregon's rainy winters and dry summers. From moss-resistant designs to earthquake-resilient hardscaping, we solve local challenges.
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Horizon Landscaping began with restoring historic Craftsman bungalows in the Hawthorne district. We specialize in solutions for damp Pacific Northwest conditions.
Our team combines horticultural knowledge with structural engineering for landscapes that endure. We prioritize native plants, proper drainage, and materials resistant to wood rot.
As active members of the Portland Urban Forestry Commission, we’re committed to sustainable practices that enhance our neighborhoods.

services.name: Rain Garden Installation
services.shortDescription: Transform drainage problems into thriving native plant displays
services.description:
Portland's average 43 inches of annual rainfall can overwhelm traditional landscapes. Our rain gardens use 12-18" deep basins filled with gravel and compost to absorb stormwater runoff naturally.

We select plants like red-twig dogwood and Douglas spirea that tolerate both temporary flooding and summer dryness. Each design includes an overflow channel to prevent basement flooding during extreme downpours.

For sloped properties in the West Hills, we incorporate terraced stone walls to slow water flow. All installations come with a 3-year plant health guarantee.
process: Step 1: Percolation test to determine soil absorption rates
Step 2: Custom design meeting with drainage engineer
Step 3: Excavation with protection for existing tree roots
Step 4: Native plant installation with mycorrhizal amendments
benefits: Eliminates standing water in low spots
Qualifies for Portland's Clean River Rewards discount

faq.question: Will rain gardens breed mosquitoes?
faq.answer: Properly designed rain gardens drain within 48 hours, preventing mosquito breeding. We include mosquito-dunk tablets in overflow areas for added protection during wet springs.

faq.question: How do they fare in drought?
faq.answer: Deep-rooted natives access water reserves other plants can't. During the 2022 drought, our rain gardens outperformed traditional lawns by 3:1 survival rates.

services.name: Historic Property Landscaping
services.shortDescription: Period-appropriate designs honoring Portland’s architectural heritage
services.description:
Restoring landscapes for Portland’s 1900s-1920s homes requires understanding historical materials and growth patterns. We source reclaimed brick for pathways matching original Craftsman color palettes and propagate heirloom roses from local specimens.

Our team documents original landscape features through city archives before designing. For Alberta Arts District bungalows, we often recreate the mixed border gardens popular in the 1910s using antique plant varieties.

All work meets Portland Historic Landmarks Commission guidelines while incorporating modern drainage solutions discreetly. We’ve successfully completed 42 historic property projects since 2018.
process: Step 1: Archival research at Portland Historical Society
Step 2: Soil remediation for lead-safe planting
Step 3: Hardscape installation with traditional mortar techniques
Step 4: Heirloom plant sourcing from specialty nurseries
benefits: Increases property value through historical accuracy
Uses durable materials proven over decades
Eligible for property tax abatement programs

faq.question: Can you match my home’s original plants?
faq.answer: We maintain relationships with specialty growers who preserve heritage cultivars. Recently identified a 1912 peony variety for a Laurelhurst home using vintage nursery catalogs.

faq.question: We conceal French drains under replicated brick edging and use permeable pavers in carriage house driveways. One Alberta Arts project hid an irrigation system within a replica 1920s garden shed.

services.name: Earthquake-Resilient Hardscaping
services.shortDescription: Beautiful outdoor living spaces prepared for seismic activity
services.description:
Portland sits in Cascadia Subduction Zone, requiring special considerations for patios and walkways. Our dry-stacked stone walls include geogrid reinforcement and expansion joints that allow movement without collapse.

For modern concrete work, we use fiber-reinforced mixes with flexible polymer additives. Flagstone patios get a 6" gravel base that absorbs minor tremors. All projects include a post-installation inspection by a licensed geotechnical engineer.

Recent work in the Pearl District demonstrates how decorative elements can also be functional - steel gabion walls double as seismic buffers while showcasing local basalt.
process: Step 1: Soil compaction testing
Step 2: Flexible base layer installation
Step 3: Movement-accommodating joint design
Step4: Post-installation engineering certification
benefits: Complies with latest Oregon building codes

faq.question: Will these features look industrial?
faq.answer: Our designs conceal technical elements - one Mt. Tabor project used decorative bronze expansion joints that became a design feature. Gabion walls get planted with cascading sedums for natural beauty.

faq.question: How does this compare cost-wise?
faq.answer: Initial investment runs 15-20% higher than standard work, but prevents average $8,000 in earthquake damage based on OSU studies of 2018 quakes.

services.name: Native Meadow Conversion
services.shortDescription: Replace thirsty lawns with drought-proof native grasslands
services.description:
Traditional lawns waste 50% more water than native alternatives in Portland’s dry summers. Our meadow conversions use Pacific Northwest grasses like blue wildrye and California oatgrass that stay green with minimal irrigation.

The process begins with solarization to remove invasive species without herbicides. We then drill-seed at precise depths for optimal germination. For urban lots near Forest Park, we include deer-resistant flowering perennials like fawn lilies.

Maintenance is minimal after establishment - just one annual mowing. Clients report 60% lower water bills and increased bird activity within the first year.
process: Step 1: Soil analysis for amendment needs
Step 2: 8-week solarization process
Step 3: Precision seeding with native mycorrhizae
Step 4: Establishment phase monitoring
benefits: Qualifies for Portland water bureau rebates

faq.question: Will this look unkempt?
faq.answer: We design structured meadows with mowed edges and intentional plant groupings. A recent Belmont project features winding stone paths through flowering areas for controlled wildness.

faq.question: How long until full establishment?
faq.answer: Most meadows reach maturity in 18-24 months. We provide seasonal care guides and offer discounted follow-up visits during the transition period.

services.name: Vertical Garden Systems
services.shortDescription: Space-efficient urban gardening for Portland’s narrow lots
services.description:
Maximize growing space in close-packed neighborhoods with our structural living walls. These self-contained systems use recycled felt planters and drip irrigation to transform blank walls into productive gardens.

For Pearl District condos, we install modular panels with built-in water reservoirs that last 5 days between fillings. Eastside industrial conversions get rugged steel frame systems for hops or kiwi vines. All include automatic pH balancing for optimal plant health.

Our proprietary soil blend combines coconut coir with biochar to prevent compaction in vertical applications. Maintenance plans include seasonal rotation of edible and ornamental plants.
process: Step 1: Structural load assessment
Step 2: Custom panel fabrication
Step 3: Irrigation system integration
Step 4: Edible plant installation
benefits: Grows 10x more food per square foot

faq.question: What grows well vertically here?
faq.answer: Strawberries, dwarf blueberries, and Cascade hops thrive in our systems. For shade walls, we recommend Oregon oxalis and ferns that naturally grow on forest cliffs.

faq.question: How is winter protection handled?
faq.answer: We install removable frost blankets with grommet systems for easy deployment. Hardy evergreens like kinnikinnick provide winter interest when edibles are dormant.

reviews.name: Mikael R.
reviews.text: Horizon restored our 1915 bungalow's original garden using archived photos from the Historical Society. They found century-old rose cultivars at a specialty nursery and replicated the gravel walkways with perfect mortar consistency. The drainage system they discreetly installed saved our basement during last winter's record rainfall.

reviews.name: Sophia K.
reviews.text: Our yard in the West Hills used to become a swamp every winter. Horizon's terraced rain garden handles the runoff beautifully while attracting hummingbirds with red-flowering currants. Three years later, the plants have filled in exactly as promised - zero maintenance beyond seasonal pruning.

reviews.name: Carlos T.
reviews.text: The gabion wall Horizon built along our slope in the Pearl hasn't budged through three minor quakes. They incorporated our existing concrete rubble into the design, saving hauling fees. The steel cages are planted with sedums that change color seasonally - functional and beautiful.

reviews.name: Priya S.
reviews.text: After years of struggling with brown lawns in summer, we converted to a native meadow in our Belmont backyard. Horizon's plant selection has something blooming from March to October. Our kids love spotting the swallowtail butterflies that now visit daily.

reviews.name: James L.
reviews.text: Our narrow lot in Alberta Arts couldn't fit a traditional garden. Horizon's living wall produces enough lettuce and herbs for our family of four, plus flowers for cutting. The automatic irrigation saved our plants during last summer's heat dome when we were away.

reviews.name: Naomi W.
reviews.text: Finding someone who understood the specific needs of our 1927 Tudor was challenging until we met Horizon. They replicated the original parterre garden using antique boxwood varieties and even matched the decomposed granite paths found in period photographs. The Historical Landmarks Commission approved their plans without revisions.

service-areas.title: Portland Metro Landscaping Service Areas
service-areas.description: Detailed information about our landscaping services in Beaverton, Hillsboro, Lake Oswego, Tigard, and surrounding communities.

serviceAreas.content: Horizon Landscaping proudly serves Beaverton, OR, a thriving city with a population of around 100,000. Many homes here date back to the 1970s and 1980s, featuring classic suburban ranch-style designs that can benefit from modern landscaping upgrades. Our team specializes in creating low-maintenance, climate-appropriate designs that thrive in Beaverton's mild, wet winters with persistent rainfall. Whether you need drainage solutions, seasonal cleanups, or a full yard transformation, we tailor our services to fit your home's unique needs.
serviceAreas.localContext: Beaverton is known for its well-established neighborhoods with older ranch-style homes, making landscaping updates a popular choice for homeowners.

serviceAreas.content: Hillsboro, OR, is a rapidly growing city with a population of approximately 110,000, driven by its tech corridor and newer housing developments. Horizon Landscaping helps homeowners and businesses create outdoor spaces that complement Hillsboro's modern aesthetic while addressing the challenges of its mild, wet winters. From drought-resistant plantings to smart irrigation systems, we ensure your landscape stays vibrant year-round. Our team is experienced in working with HOAs and newer construction properties to meet community standards.
serviceAreas.localContext: Hillsboro's tech-driven growth has led to an influx of newer homes, often requiring landscaping that balances beauty and functionality.

serviceAreas.content: Lake Oswego, OR, is an upscale community of about 40,000 residents, known for its stunning lakefront properties and meticulously maintained landscapes. Horizon Landscaping specializes in high-end designs that enhance the natural beauty of Lake Oswego's waterfront and hillside homes. We address the unique challenges of the area's mild, wet winters, offering solutions like erosion control, waterfront plantings, and luxury hardscaping. Our team understands the importance of curb appeal in this prestigious market.
serviceAreas.localContext: Lake Oswego's affluent neighborhoods demand premium landscaping that complements its scenic lakefront and hilly terrain.

serviceAreas.content: Tigard, OR, is a family-friendly city with around 55,000 residents, featuring many 1970s-80s suburban homes that could use landscaping updates. Horizon Landscaping helps Tigard homeowners transform their outdoor spaces with designs that suit the area's mild, wet winters. Whether you're looking to add drought-tolerant plants, improve drainage, or create a more functional backyard, our team delivers personalized solutions. We also work with local businesses to enhance commercial properties with attractive, low-maintenance landscapes.
serviceAreas.localContext: Tigard's older suburban homes often need landscaping upgrades to improve functionality and curb appeal.

serviceAreas.content: Horizon Landscaping serves Tualatin, OR, a charming city with a mix of established neighborhoods and newer developments. Located along the Tualatin River, this area benefits from our expertise in water-wise landscaping and erosion control. We design landscapes that thrive in the region's mild, wet winters while requiring minimal upkeep. From riverfront properties to suburban yards, we create outdoor spaces that enhance your home's value and beauty.
serviceAreas.localContext: Tualatin's proximity to the river and blend of old and new homes make it ideal for customized landscaping solutions.

serviceAreas.content: West Linn, OR, is an affluent community known for its scenic views and upscale homes. Horizon Landscaping specializes in creating elegant, sustainable landscapes that complement West Linn's natural beauty. We address the challenges of the area's mild, wet winters with solutions like terracing for hillside properties and native plantings for low-maintenance beauty. Our team is experienced in working with high-end properties to deliver exceptional results.
serviceAreas.localContext: West Linn's hilly terrain and luxurious homes require sophisticated landscaping designs that harmonize with the environment.
